title: What If Letterboxd Ratings Decided the Best Picture Oscar?
text: Jason Kottke explores a hypothetical scenario where Letterboxd's contemporary user ratings, rather than Academy votes, determined Best Picture winners from 1927-2023. The results would differ dramatically from actual Oscar outcomes. The analysis notes that you'd get lots more foreign films from directors like Ozu, Kurosawa, Truffaut, Leone, Bergman, and Tarkovsky. Notable alternate winners mentioned include The Empire Strikes Back (over Ordinary People), Do the Right Thing (over Driving Miss Daisy), Brokeback Mountain (over Crash), and Paddington 2. The article highlights 1999 as an illustrative example. The actual Oscar nominees were American Beauty, The Cider House Rules, The Green Mile, The Insider, and The Sixth Sense. By Letterboxd ratings that year, the top-rated films included Fight Club, The Iron Giant, The Green Mile, Magnolia, and All About My Mother—demonstrating significant divergence in audience preference versus Academy selection.
